---
title: "CB3 Equity Report — Public Services Maps"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
jupyter: cb3
---

## Purpose

This notebook reads the clean Public Services outputs from
`scripts/public_service/build.py` and maps three dimensions:

1. Park facility inventory: one categorical point map, one marker per park
   per facility type it contains (`park_facility_inventory`).
2. Public transportation access: one donut marker per tract, sized by total
   transit stops and split into wedges by subway entrances vs. bus stops
   (`subway_entrance_count`, `bus_stop_count`). Quarter-mile subway
   accessibility (`subway_access_pct`) is computed and shown as a tooltip
   field rather than its own choropleth.
3. Public transportation access (points): the same subway entrances and bus
   stops as individual points, not aggregated to a tract (`transit_stop_points`).
4. Public restrooms: a standalone categorical point map by operational
   status and accessibility (`public_restrooms`).

Each map also includes the shared demographic layers (income, age, race and
ethnicity, poverty, limited-English-proficient households) as a selectable
choropleth backdrop, so any Public Services dimension can be compared against
demographic context directly.

Map HTML files are written to `maps/public_service/`.

```{python}
# Import packages for tract data, geometry, and interactive maps.
from pathlib import Path
import sys
import yaml

import folium
import geopandas as gpd
import pandas as pd

# Ensure the reusable scripts package imports whether this QMD is rendered from
# the project root or from scripts/.
for candidate in [Path.cwd(), *Path.cwd().parents]:
    if (candidate / "src" / "map_utils.py").exists():
        sys.path.insert(0, str(candidate))
        break

from src.map_utils import (
    add_categorical_point_layer,
    add_demographic_backdrop_layers,
    add_map_title,
    add_zero_value_legend,
    build_grouped_bubble_map,
    find_project_dir,
    make_base_map,
)
```

```{python}
# Define project inputs and output locations.
PROJECT_DIR = find_project_dir()
CLEAN_DIR = PROJECT_DIR / "data" / "clean"
TRACT_PATH = CLEAN_DIR / "public_service_tract.csv"
PARK_POINTS_PATH = CLEAN_DIR / "public_service_park_points.csv"
PARK_FACILITY_POINTS_PATH = CLEAN_DIR / "public_service_park_facility_points.csv"
TRANSIT_POINTS_PATH = CLEAN_DIR / "public_service_transit_points.csv"
RESTROOM_POINTS_PATH = CLEAN_DIR / "public_service_restroom_points.csv"
TRACT_GEOMETRY_PATH = PROJECT_DIR / "data" / "raw" / "Geography" / "2020_Census_Tracts_20260505.geojson"
MAP_OUTPUT_DIR = PROJECT_DIR / "maps" / "public_service"
MAP_OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

with open(PROJECT_DIR / "docs" / "metric_metadata.yml") as f:
    ALL_METRICS = yaml.safe_load(f)
```

## Prepare tract geometry and metrics

```{python}
# Read the clean tract metrics with GEOID preserved as an 11-character string.
metrics = pd.read_csv(TRACT_PATH, dtype={"GEOID": "string"})

# Read the 2020 tract polygons and keep only the 31 CB3 tracts.
tract_geometry = gpd.read_file(TRACT_GEOMETRY_PATH)
tract_geometry["GEOID"] = tract_geometry["geoid"].astype("string").str.zfill(11)
tract_geometry = tract_geometry[["GEOID", "geometry"]]

tracts = tract_geometry.merge(metrics, on="GEOID", how="inner", validate="one_to_one")
tracts = gpd.GeoDataFrame(tracts, geometry="geometry", crs=tract_geometry.crs)

assert len(tracts) == 31
assert tracts["GEOID"].is_unique
assert set(tracts["GEOID"]) == set(metrics["GEOID"])

# Read tract-level demographics for the shared selectable backdrop layers.
baseline_profile = pd.read_csv(CLEAN_DIR / "cb3_tract_baseline_profile_clean.csv", dtype={"geoid": "string"})
baseline_profile["GEOID"] = baseline_profile["geoid"].str.zfill(11)

DEMOGRAPHIC_COLUMNS = [
    "GEOID",
    "median_household_income",
    "age_0_to_19_share",
    "age_20_to_64_share",
    "age_65_plus_share",
    "white_non_hispanic_share",
    "black_non_hispanic_share",
    "asian_non_hispanic_share",
    "hispanic_share",
    "poverty_rate",
    "lep_household_share",
]
tracts = tracts.merge(baseline_profile[DEMOGRAPHIC_COLUMNS], on="GEOID", how="left", validate="one_to_one")

# Park real footprint polygons, reconstructed from their WKT column.
park_points_raw = pd.read_csv(PARK_POINTS_PATH, dtype={"GEOID": "string"})
park_polygons = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    park_points_raw,
    geometry=gpd.GeoSeries.from_wkt(park_points_raw["geometry_wkt"]),
    crs="EPSG:4326",
)

# Park x facility-type points, transit points, and restroom points keep
# their own native coordinates; attach tract/NTA labels for tooltips.
park_facility_points = pd.read_csv(PARK_FACILITY_POINTS_PATH, dtype={"GEOID": "string"})
transit_points = pd.read_csv(TRANSIT_POINTS_PATH, dtype={"GEOID": "string"})
restroom_points = pd.read_csv(RESTROOM_POINTS_PATH, dtype={"GEOID": "string"})
```

```{python}
# Build METRIC_SPECS and DEMOGRAPHIC_SPECS from metric_metadata.yml.
def _to_map_spec(meta, include_filename=False):
    spec = {
        "dimension": meta["short_label"],
        "label":     meta["label"],
        "unit":      meta["unit"],
        "palette":   meta.get("palette"),
    }
    if include_filename:
        spec["filename"] = meta["filename"]
    for key in ("positive_values_only", "zero_color", "zero_label", "category_colors", "fill_color", "outline_color"):
        if key in meta:
            spec[key] = meta[key]
    return spec


METRIC_SPECS = {
    var: _to_map_spec(meta, include_filename=True)
    for var, meta in ALL_METRICS.items()
    if meta["domain"] == "public_service"
}

EXCLUDED_DEMOGRAPHIC_METRICS = {"senior_poverty_rate"}
DEMOGRAPHIC_SPECS = {
    var: _to_map_spec(meta)
    for var, meta in ALL_METRICS.items()
    if meta["domain"] == "demographics" and var not in EXCLUDED_DEMOGRAPHIC_METRICS
}

for metric in ["subway_access_pct", "subway_entrance_count", "bus_stop_count"]:
    assert metric in tracts.columns, f"{metric} missing from tracts"
for metric in DEMOGRAPHIC_SPECS:
    assert metric in tracts.columns, f"{metric} missing from tracts"
```

## Park facility inventory

```{python}
# Real park footprint polygons as a plain outline/fill (white outline, black
# fill, matching the bubble-layer style used elsewhere), with a facility-type
# icon layer inside each footprint (icons spread around a small ring rather
# than stacked at one point when a park has more than one facility type —
# see offset_icon_points_ft in build.py). Small parks (many gardens/triangles
# are under 0.1 acres) are too small for icons to sit legibly inside; icons
# there will visibly spill past the park's edges.
footprint_spec = METRIC_SPECS["park_footprint"]
facility_spec = METRIC_SPECS["park_facility_inventory"]
facility_output_path = MAP_OUTPUT_DIR / facility_spec["filename"]

facility_map = make_base_map(tracts)
add_demographic_backdrop_layers(facility_map, tracts, DEMOGRAPHIC_SPECS)

park_polygon_layer_data = park_polygons[["park_name", "category", "facility_list", "geometry"]]
folium.GeoJson(
    data=park_polygon_layer_data.to_json(),
    name=f"Park footprints (n={len(park_polygons)})",
    overlay=True,
    show=True,
    style_function=lambda feature: {
        "fillColor": footprint_spec["fill_color"],
        "color": footprint_spec["outline_color"],
        "weight": 1.5,
        "fillOpacity": 0.65,
    },
    highlight_function=lambda _: {"weight": 3, "fillOpacity": 0.85},
    tooltip=folium.GeoJsonTooltip(
        fields=["park_name", "category", "facility_list"],
        aliases=["Park", "Category", "Facilities"],
        sticky=False,
    ),
).add_to(facility_map)

add_categorical_point_layer(
    facility_map,
    park_facility_points,
    category_col="facility_type",
    category_colors=facility_spec["category_colors"],
    name=f"Park facility icons (n={len(park_facility_points)})",
    lat_col="latitude",
    lon_col="longitude",
    tooltip_fields=["facility_type", "park_name", "tract_label"],
    tooltip_aliases=["Facility", "Park", "Census tract"],
    size=12,
)
add_map_title(
    facility_map,
    "CB3 Public Services: Park facility inventory",
    "Choropleth: use the layer control to pick a demographic fill. Park shapes: "
    "footprint outline/fill. Diamonds: one icon per facility type inside each park "
    "(spread around a ring, not exact placement, on parks with more than one type). "
    "Pickleball courts, chess/game tables, and skate parks are not in the source "
    "inventory and are not shown.",
)
add_zero_value_legend(facility_map, "#d9d9d9", "Demographic data not available")
folium.LayerControl(collapsed=False, position="topright").add_to(facility_map)
facility_map.save(facility_output_path)
facility_map
```

## Public transportation access

```{python}
# One donut marker per tract, sized by total transit stops (subway entrances
# + bus stops) and split into wedges by their share, over the shared
# selectable demographic choropleth backdrop. Subway entrances and bus stops
# commonly both occur in the same tract, so separate same-coordinate bubble
# layers would stack and hide each other; combine_as_pie=True draws one
# marker instead.
transit_output_path = MAP_OUTPUT_DIR / METRIC_SPECS["subway_entrance_count"]["filename"]

tracts["subway_access_display"] = tracts["subway_access_pct"].round(1).astype(str) + "%"

transit_map = build_grouped_bubble_map(
    tracts, METRIC_SPECS, DEMOGRAPHIC_SPECS,
    ["subway_entrance_count", "bus_stop_count"],
    title="CB3 Public Services: Public transportation access",
    subtitle="Choropleth: use the layer control to pick a demographic fill. Donut "
    "markers: size shows total transit stops per tract; wedges show its mix of "
    "subway entrances and bus stops.",
    output_path=transit_output_path,
    tooltip_fields=["tract_label", "nta_name", "subway_access_display"],
    tooltip_aliases=["Census tract", "NTA", "Tract area within 1/4 mile of a subway entrance"],
    combine_as_pie=True,
    pie_name="Public transportation access",
    pie_legend_title="Transit stop type",
)
transit_map
```

## Public transportation access (points)

```{python}
# Alternate, less-aggregated view of the same data: individual subway
# entrance and bus stop points rather than tract-level pie bubbles.
transit_points_spec = METRIC_SPECS["transit_stop_points"]
transit_points_output_path = MAP_OUTPUT_DIR / transit_points_spec["filename"]

transit_points_map = make_base_map(tracts)
add_demographic_backdrop_layers(transit_points_map, tracts, DEMOGRAPHIC_SPECS)
add_categorical_point_layer(
    transit_points_map,
    transit_points,
    category_col="stop_type",
    category_colors=transit_points_spec["category_colors"],
    name=f"Transit stops (n={len(transit_points)})",
    lat_col="latitude",
    lon_col="longitude",
    tooltip_fields=["stop_name", "entrance_type", "tract_label"],
    tooltip_aliases=["Stop", "Entrance type", "Census tract"],
    size=10,
)
add_map_title(
    transit_points_map,
    "CB3 Public Services: Public transportation access (points)",
    "Choropleth: use the layer control to pick a demographic fill. Markers: individual "
    "subway station entrances and bus stops, not aggregated to a tract.",
)
add_zero_value_legend(transit_points_map, "#d9d9d9", "Demographic data not available")
folium.LayerControl(collapsed=False, position="topright").add_to(transit_points_map)
transit_points_map.save(transit_points_output_path)
transit_points_map
```

## Public restrooms

```{python}
# Standalone categorical point map by operational status and accessibility.
restroom_spec = METRIC_SPECS["public_restrooms"]
restroom_output_path = MAP_OUTPUT_DIR / restroom_spec["filename"]

restroom_map = make_base_map(tracts)
add_demographic_backdrop_layers(restroom_map, tracts, DEMOGRAPHIC_SPECS)
add_categorical_point_layer(
    restroom_map,
    restroom_points,
    category_col="status_category",
    category_colors=restroom_spec["category_colors"],
    name=f"Public restrooms (n={len(restroom_points)})",
    lat_col="latitude",
    lon_col="longitude",
    tooltip_fields=["facility_name", "location_type", "tract_label"],
    tooltip_aliases=["Facility", "Location type", "Census tract"],
    size=15,
)
add_map_title(
    restroom_map,
    "CB3 Public Services: Public restrooms",
    "Choropleth: use the layer control to pick a demographic fill. Markers: colored "
    "by operational status and, if operational, ADA accessibility. Not in "
    "CB3_Data_Reference_by_Domain_v4.csv's Public Services rows; shown here as a "
    "standalone map from the available clean data.",
)
add_zero_value_legend(restroom_map, "#d9d9d9", "Demographic data not available")
folium.LayerControl(collapsed=False, position="topright").add_to(restroom_map)
restroom_map.save(restroom_output_path)
restroom_map
```

## Output validation

```{python}
# Verify that all HTML deliverables were written.
expected_map_files = [
    facility_output_path,
    transit_output_path,
    transit_points_output_path,
    restroom_output_path,
]

for map_file in expected_map_files:
    assert map_file.exists()
    assert map_file.stat().st_size > 10_000

print(f"Wrote {len(expected_map_files)} map files to {MAP_OUTPUT_DIR}")
```
